generated_description: An adversary executed a custom tool with elevated privileges to conduct discovery operations within a domain. This tool was specifically used to query Active Directory object access control lists, targeting the 'Domain Admins' group to identify its permissions or members. To hinder forensic analysis and remove traces of activity, the adversary cleared system event logs and deleted the custom tool from the compromised system.

Adversaries may use SID-History Injection to escalate privileges and bypass access controls. The Windows security identifier (SID) is a unique value that identifies a user or group account. generated_description: An adversary executed a utility from the command line at an elevated integrity level to perform reconnaissance against Active Directory. This involved querying object access control lists for privileged domain groups, leveraging internal network protocols to interact with the directory service. Following the reconnaissance, the adversary deleted temporary files created during the activity, likely to remove forensic artifacts and evade detection.
